title: Design collision-safe cache keys
description: Derive keys from every value-affecting input with a version prefix so distinct requests never collide.
body: Use when defining how a cache key is built from a request. Do not concatenate raw user input into keys — collisions return another user's data and unbounded variants explode the key space. Actions: include every parameter that changes the value plus a schema/version prefix, normalize ordering and casing, and namespace by entity type. Validation signals include no observed cross-tenant leakage and a bounded distinct-key count under load. Risk boundary: a key that omits a varying input serves wrong data silently — treat key correctness as a security invariant and require review.
